Alright, who got a couple good ideas from that session? I can only get 250,000 leads from that. Like, I want to go in that market. I just don't know anything about guns. It scares me. I'm just joking. Yeah, the potato gun market. Who wants some free potatoes? Alright. Okay. Alright. Okay, so I'm not going to spend a ton of time on this tonight because this is something that it's more of a concept than what you understand and then it's something that you got to do a little research and effort on your side to be able to implement. And so I just want to help make sure you bridge the gap and really understand kind of what the concept and then when you do it becomes really, really easy, right? Okay, so we talked again. We got our friend in Funnels, which again, I'm not talking about those during this event at all, but starting Monday we'll be going into your break even funnel, right? From there we've drilled bridge pages to bring people in, to bring cold traffic into our world. Let me step back here. This is what we're talking about right now is content. So I told you guys before that the networks, these networks up here, right? Facebook, Twitter, YouTube, all these things, like they don't want us marketers just selling stuff as much as we want. So the evolution, and we've talked about this a lot today, is coming to the spot where we create content for these networks, then push people to our bridge pages, which then push people into our funnels. Okay. Now I want to caveat this by saying one thing is that sometimes I push people directly from here all the way here. I skip a bridge page. Sometimes there's not something where it has to be this, this, this, and this is marketing. This is, it's like, there's like 10% science and like 90% creativity and trying things, right? It's this whole like throwing stuff against the wall. And so it doesn't have to be, you know, Russell said it has to go from Facebook ad to a quiz to a this, to a this, like sometimes, yeah, but it's something like I'm trying to give you guys tools that you can then use and you're going to try different things. And maybe I try a quiz page to a video seller, maybe it's a quiz page to, you know, or maybe it's things like that. So I want you to understand this is a framework. It's not like it has to be this, this, or this, right? That's not how it works in this, in this world. It's we're dealing with people. So what we give you tools, the things we know work and say, how can I, what can I, like what order can I put this in for my business for what I'm trying to do? Okay. All right. So what I want to talk about now is, is this part here, this is the content, this is the platform. Okay. And there's a lot of platforms out there and there's more coming out every single day, right? We've got Facebook, we know about this Twitter, there's YouTube, there's Periscope, there's Instagram, there's WordPress. There's places out there that, that people are already going for content. Okay. One of the big things I learned from one of my early mentors, his name is Steven Pierce. He talked about traffic and he said, you know, everyone's out there trying to figure out how do I create traffic? I need to get traffic to my website. He's like, you got to understand, like, you don't have to create traffic. Like people are already online. People are already there. Traffic is happening. You don't have to create. All you have to do is you have to figure out a way, you see this big stream of traffic. How do you like step out in front of it and get some of that coming to you, right? It's already there. Okay. You're just finding it and you're, you're getting out in front of it. So that's a lot of what this whole concept is about is we're finding like, where, where's our audience at? Where are the people we want to, we want to work with? Where are they at? Like obviously they're on Facebook, right? They're probably on Twitter. They're probably, they're in these spots already. So we've got to create things that come out, that grab them and grab their attention to get them to want to come into, into our world, right? Um, uh, like their, their gun offers is a perfect example. Like from email lists, from Facebook, from social media, like, hey, when, when you're free gun and ammo, like that brings a lot of people, a lot of people in, right? So that's kind of what we're, what we're talking about. Now, um, this is from the dot com secrets book chapter one. Um, should we have story time? I can read it. Just kidding. So the first thing I talk about in the book is this concept of the secret formula. You guys all remember this? If not, you need to read the book again. I'm just kidding. Well, you should. I like that book. I think it's really good. All right. So the secret formula, I want to kind of recap it for you because this is kind of the framework of what, when you understand this piece, how it ties into the content, it should make everything like, oh wow, restless, all makes like perfect sense now. Right. So this is the framework. So the first thing we have to ask when we are in this business is who do we want to serve? How many of you guys have very clearly defined, you know, exactly who it is that you want to serve and to sell things to? That's good. That's a lot more than I would have expected. So that's good. When I got in this business, I did not know. I just jumped out. I started creating things and selling things. And then like one day I woke up and I had a whole bunch of customers I couldn't stand. Brent and I were talking about this during lunch. Like my seminar is five or six years ago, I would show up and I'd be like sick to my stomach because I just didn't enjoy the people I was around. In the very first Ignite event we did, man, a year and a half ago, how many of you guys were at that event? You know, Anthony was there, Bonnie was there. Anyway, that was the first event I had done like five years ago. I was like, I'm never doing events again. I don't want to talk to people. Like I was so scared. So when we kind of, we restructured our business and we said consciously, like who do we actually want to serve? We want to serve entrepreneurs that are successful. They're doing these kind of things. And we launched the dot com secrets labs book, right? That's how we got these people in and everything. And we did our first event afterwards. I remember coming to that event being sick to my stomach the morning I got there. And I showed up and I was just like, oh man, like, because I remember how much pain I had before. And I got that event and like everyone was awesome. Like the whole audience, like it was just, it was so different. I remember when it went to lunch that day and Brent was like, have you noticed anything different? I'm like, yeah, like everyone in there is awesome. And it all came back to us kind of changing who our who is, right? Like that's why I do these, what do we do? Three times a year events kind of like this now because like our who is different than people who show up. Like you guys are funded. Like you all have cool businesses. You're cool people. Like I'm not just saying that to flatter you. Like it's true. Like I wouldn't keep doing these events if you guys drove me crazy, but you guys are all awesome. So it all comes down to this. Like we figured out who our who was, who do we want to work with? And that was step number one. So if you haven't figured out, like you needed to find that because if you don't nine times out of 10, you're going to get the wrong people coming into your into your world. And there will be a day when you wake up where you're just like, wow, what did I create? I don't want to do this anymore. Like this is not, this is not who I'm passionate about serving. Right? You got to figure out who that person is first off. Okay. After you know who that who is and after you very, very clearly find that is the second step is where are those people at right now? Okay. And we got to find those people are at. The first time I think I got this is about 10 years ago. I was thinking about this one day and I was like, you know, it's kind of like, I like using the word congregations when I think about this. Like we hear where congregation, what do you typically think of? Church, right? And so I was thinking like, if I was selling something and let's just say I was selling something, let me step back. Like, like we know in churches, people congregate based on similar beliefs and values, right? And they do every single week. So every single week, all of the Muslims gathered together over here and the Mormons gather together here and the Catholics over here and the Baptist over here and everyone self congregates based on their beliefs and their values, right? And so they're there. They're already like in a spot. And so if I was selling something to Mormons, where would I go? They're all right there. They're just sitting there in one building at one time every single week. Like that's where I should go to sell them to, right? You know, I understand I'm not telling you guys to sell things at churches. That's not my goal. But what's cool about the internet is the exact same thing has happened online. That's the power of what the internet has given us is this given us this chance where like think about the things that interest you right now. There's some weird things that all of you guys are interested in, I'm betting, right? The internet has given us a chance where like let's say I'm into potato guns, right? I can hang out with all the other people in the world that are into potato guns all in one spot. We can talk about it. We can hang out. And that's what the internet has given us is we have the ability to do that. And I think about in each of your lives there's probably four or five like things that you're interested in and you're in these little communities, am I right? I think about for me like things that interest me like the whole biohacking community is really interesting to me. So I'm in those, I'm in the Facebookers, I'm on the email list, I get the things I buy there, supplement. It's like that world is really interesting to me. Like that's the subculture that I'm part of, right? The internet marketing world, that's the subculture I'm very passionate about and I'm part of. I have a religious thing that I'm very part of and I'm in these different subcultures because they're interesting to me, right? And so you think about this like that's happening all over the internet in every one of your businesses that people are already self congregating themselves together. They're somewhere, they're already in a spot. And so what we have to figure out is just where are they at? That's it, we just gotta figure out where are those people at? When I was wrestling at Boise State just down the road, I remember every single night, the student athletes aren't typically the smartest ones and the wrestlers are like the bottom of the rung usually, right? And so we had mandatory study hall. You had to do like an hour or two hours of study hall every single night, otherwise you pretty much failed. We couldn't pass classes without it, right? So we had to go to study hall every night. I remember one day like six months into going to Boise State, I remember sitting there in study hall looking around and I was on the site called themat.com and I'm talking about wrestling and hanging out and looking at stuff. I look around and I notice that every single other wrestler at study hall, we're all on themat.com, all of us. I was like, how funny, I bet you that every college campus in America and in Russia and in Germany and every other, we're all on the exact same website. Every wrestler today right now is on that site talking about it. It's a congregation. I was like, if I was selling wrestling stuff, I'd just go right here, everyone's right there, right? And so for you, when you identify who you're who is, the second thing you gotta figure out is where exactly are those people at? If you don't know that, that's the key to all these traffic problems, all these things, how do we get people in? That's the key to figure out. And it's not just what Facebook groups are they in, but what forums are on, what email lists are they on, what type of news do they, there's so many things you can figure out, right? So that's next, is the where. If you're like, what's the bait that's going to get that person to raise their hand? Sometimes the bait's like a free plus shipping offer, sometimes it's a webinar, sometimes it's a pre-frame bridge. This can be a lot of different things. For the purposes of this session right now, my bait is content. So what do I need to write? What video do I need to make? What podcast, whatever platform you're going to, do I need to create so that my dream people, when they see that, raise your hand and say, oh, I'm going to read that, I've got to find out what's happening. Because as soon as I can get them to raise their hand and take the bait, boom, they read the content, the content pushes them to a pre-frame bridge and there's the funnel and this process is warming them up along the way. And then from there, that's where the funnel's now come in and we send them up through our value ladder and everything else. So this is the key. So again, for today's conversation, the bait that we're focusing on is content. And so it's kind of the bottom of the value ladder here before anything else is happening. It's the first interaction they have with you and this is if you can give them value, at that point then they're going to want more. I haven't talked a lot today about value ladder. I've read the book, we go deep into it there. But the value ladder is like when people receive value at any rung here, they naturally want more. They will naturally keep us sending up. You guys are in this room today because you probably received some kind of value from me or my team or my company or something. Otherwise, there's no way you would have paid what you paid and flew to Boise, Idaho to be here. And at least it's warm outside right now. The next event's in January. We'll see how much value I provided today by how many of you guys come back in January, right? Where it's really cool. But if you provide value here, then people will send up. So this is like my content. If I provide value, you're listening, you're like, dang, I'm in. I need more of this. That's the goal. Okay? All right. So there's lots of different networks, right? And how many of you guys have read the book? It's a jab jab hook or jab jab, what's it called? Jab jab jab hook? Nobody knows. So Gary Vaynerchuk wrote a book called Jab Jab Jab Punch or something like that, right? And who's read the book by the way? I bought it. I haven't read it. How many of us did that fit anyway? So the book was really good. But the one like the big a-ha I had in the book is I was thinking about myself, right? And I had this webinar, right? And this is where I make it all my money. So I'm going out of Facebook. I'm jamming a webinar down people's throats. I'm going to Twitter and I'm jamming a webinar and I'm going to Instagram and I'm jamming a webinar. And people are just jamming this thing down people's throats because that's all I want to sell. And that's how my life's been. That's what we do. That's what most of us are doing, right? Like we know what we want to sell so we're just jamming it down each network's throat. And what that book gave me the understanding was, he said, look, every single network that you're looking at has a different, the way that they, like what they're doing is different, right? When you're on Facebook, think about the things that you click on. What is it? Right? When you're scrolling through your fan, what's it called? Your news feed and you're bored out of your mind and you're, whatever, you're going through all this pain and you're trying to figure out something. Like what are the things you click on? But you got to think about that. What is it that give people a click in Facebook? Okay? Because it's different if you go over to Instagram, right? Instagram's got limitations. You got, what is it, videos that are 15 seconds or 30 seconds or something like that, right? Okay? You go to Twitter, you got like what, 170 characters? You go to Periscope, you got this video. You go, like everywhere's got different ways that people are consuming that content. Us as marketers, we can try to jam things down but if we want it to work for us, we have to meet them where they're at. So when we decide, hey, I'm going to go to Instagram and to market there, I need to look at like how are people at Instagram consuming content? Like I need to see what that process is and understand it and I've got to create content that that audience is looking for. Okay? And this is kind of a big off of me. It's really understanding that. And so that's kind of the first thing I want to mention, right? So I'm going to kind of go through some of these networks and again, I'm not going to give you guys the solution today. I just want to raise the question today. So when you guys decide, because most of you guys aren't going to go like, I'm going to advertise in 30 different networks, okay? None of us should. Like we're being very deliberate about this right now. Like we are taking small steps and here's the, here's someone we want to put time and effort into and so we study and figure out what they're doing and how they're doing it and then we build the things around it. It's not going to benefit any of you guys to go and like, let's jump into 10 networks at once because you'll just, nothing will ever happen, right? So ideally it's going to be you picking one starting there until you get it working and then you can pick another one. So I want to kind of just show you guys a couple of them to start thinking through. Then it's going to be on your side. Like if you decide this is what you want to commit to is then learning and really understanding that world, right? So first thing I want to show here is Facebook. Now like Gary, like when I saw this video, it like, I just like, this guy was right. Like his book, everything he said was perfect. So I'm on my phone flipping through and all of a sudden I see this video and now your video is like autoplay the first five seconds, but it's quiet, right? So Gary is like, the first like five seconds and I'm like, oh, I click on the button and all of a sudden it starts playing. I'm like, dude, he built this video for Facebook. He understands that Facebook servers are going to see this thing moving around. I'm like, the stupid videos I post on Facebook, like I'm going through my pitch and things are happening. Also they click it in the middle of my pitch and they missed the whole point, right? Like he's smart to understand like what's happening to a Facebook user is sitting on their phone, probably on the bathroom, scrolling through like this, waiting. And all of a sudden it's like, this guy is yelling at me, stop, stop, stop. Like he's understanding like who that person on that network is, what they're doing and how to interact with them, right? And I was just like, like he obviously has got to explain it to me initially through the book, but like he gets like, that's it, right? Like that's how we communicate in that network. Okay. All right. Next one is YouTube. That's an embarrassing picture. Okay, so the YouTube network, this is something we're playing with right now and we're having a lot of success. We're averaging about $7 per webinar registered right now, which is a little higher. On Facebook we were closer to $3 to $3.5. So it's a little more expensive, but it's working and so it's really exciting for us right now. And so there's different, again on Facebook there's different promotion strategies. We're going to go to the most tomorrow. YouTube is different promotion strategies, but conceptually what I want to understand is the cool thing about YouTube is that people come to YouTube and there's a bajillion videos, right? But what's cool is that you can go and you can find people in your space. Like for example, there are probably 100,000 videos of people doing demos on how to use lead pages, right? And what's cool, I can buy ads on that video. So they watch my video before they watch the how to use the lead pages video. Okay, there's a whole bunch of videos about Infusionsoft, like how to use Infusionsoft, how to set your campaign builder, how to blah, blah, blah, blah, all these things. And all these videos are there and they're getting, you know, 100,000 visitors or views a month or a year, whatever that is. And I can buy ads directly on there. Okay, so if I understand that, I need to like step into the view, right? So some dudes on lead pages, he realizes how bad it sucks. He's like, this is the worst thing on planet earth. Let me go to YouTube and see if anyone can show me how to move an image from here to here, right? All right, so then he goes to YouTube, types in how to move an image in lead pages or whatever, right? Click a button and the video pops up. And right before that video pops up. So you're here watching a video about lead pages, right? Well if you already love lead pages, then click on the skip button right now. If you want to see something that's amazing, then hold on. Cause I'm going to show you something that we call click funnels. All right, now click funnels is a tool we built because we hated how lead pages gives you a page and you're stuck. You can't move things around. You can't really edit things. You can change headlines and that's about it. And so we built a tool that gives you the ability to build anything you can dream of, but it's really simple and easy to use. And I wanted to do something really cool for you today because we, we know that you're interested in creating pages, but you want something better, I'm guessing, right? So I'm going to give you guys a gift. First thing I'm doing a webinar this week, that's actually going to show you a sales funnel that we built inside of click funnels. And you can register for this webinar for free. So I'm going to cost you anything. I'm going to show you that sales funnel, show you all the pages and what we did and why we did it and how we get traffic to it. Now the other thing to also think about too is, well this YouTube strategy is not perfect for this one but let's do the blog one. So next one is blogging, right? So I've been trying for years to have a blog. I hired Neil Patel because I thought if I pay someone 25 grand to come out for one day and talk about blogging I'll probably start blogging, right? That was like my, I hope I do. So it's really hard. So for me it was kind of the same thing with blogging. I feel like I had to figure out my voice. Just like video, you figure out your voice. What's your voice in blogging? And my biggest problem was I never had one so I never did it. And after we finished the Dotcom stickers book where we have little doodle sketches and then concepts, I realized that's the way I speak. That's my thing and so we haven't done a ton of these yet but kind of what I'm doing now is I'm taking concepts, we're sketching them out and we have a blog post around those, right? So you'll start seeing these hopefully a couple of times a week coming from us and they'll be going on the blog and part of it is for my true fans, right? Trying to get those guys more engaged and more excited. So that's step number one. But step number two now and we'll talk more about the strategy tomorrow but after that's done and the blog post is finished, there's tons of things I can do to get that blog post out, right? Taking it and Jesse's going to be sharing with you guys a really cool strategy. He's been doing it for Brendan Bouchard and just his company is blowing up from this one strategy that Jesse's doing. He just started doing it for us over the last what, 30 days, 40 days, something like that? 40 days. So he's doing it for us now, it's awesome and so he's going to go through the whole strategy tomorrow and it's like taking your blog post, taking your YouTube videos, taking all these content you're creating and like how do we amplify now? How do we promote it? How do we get it so that it's not just like eight people that read your blog that see it but how do you get it so that 800,000 people see it, right? That's the goal. That's what tomorrow is all about. How do we amplify the stuff? Because posting on YouTube you're like sweet some people in YouTube saw it. How do we take that and just 10X or 20X or 100X? So that's what tomorrow is all about. Today's all about understanding the process and understanding the content creation, right? So the blog is done so you figure out like whatever your voice in your blog, maybe again it's a video blog or whatever but you create that blog and after it's done now we can take that and like for me I did these images very on purpose, right? Like it's an image on the bottom in the image it has like want more, read the blog, get the book. So there's hard code of links in every image. I want people sharing these, right? So I take this, I promote it on Facebook. I take that image, I can promote it on Instagram. And I can start spreading out this piece of content in all different directions to get more and more eyeballs to see that which then brings more people back into my world, okay? And so again that's something, the promotion side will be tomorrow but just looking for your blog, like again coming back to the figuring out your voice, like what's your voice? How do you want to talk to people? How do you want to do that? Two things I want to mention on this before I go on. This just comes back to the consistency, like the key with the consistency. We talked about with podcasting, we talked about Neil Patel when we had him and we were talking about blogging, just like you've got a blog every single day. There's another guy that's really inspiring to me, his name is Eric Worre. Can you guys follow Eric or see him? So a couple of you guys. So Eric's in the network marketing industry and first time I saw him I'm like he's a rather average individual, like he's just an average dude, like little overweight, bald, like just, I'm like okay there's this guy, right? I was just like okay, this Eric Worre. And the first one I found out about him was because he was putting on a live event and he had 10,000 people in the room. A little while later he did a webinar with Tony Robbins, he had over 100,000 people to register for it. I'm like what's this guy doing? Because there's something he's doing. I got to figure out what this is. So I started looking deeper and deeper and I had some friends that know him and what they said is that, and again I don't know if this may be myth or legend, I don't know, but it's still pretty powerful. So he went, I don't know, five or six years ago and he hired Gary Vaynerchuk. It was like Gary, I saw you do the Wine Library TV, you blew up the whole thing. How do you, like what's the secret? And Gary's like you got to do a video podcast every day. Okay, and then what? Just do it every single day. Is that it? Yeah, every day, consistently. And for the first year you're going to make nothing, after that you'll be rich. And so Eric's like alright sweet man, and for every day for five years Eric has posted a video. You go to the site, you'll see every day for, like consistently every single day for five years he's posted a video. Like I said this guy is not charismatic, he's not like, but he's consistent. Every single day and now five years later, I mean can you imagine having a seminar with 10,000 people? He could have sold a whole lot more tickets than that, that's the biggest the conference room could hold. 100,000 people registering for your webinars? If I'm closing at 15% I'd be 150,000 people times a thousand bucks. Anyone know the math on that? It's a lot of money, right? Okay, Neil Patel, 1700 opt-ins a day, consistency, right? Like some of these things sound like a pain in the butt and they are and I get it, but if you can figure out in your life like how do I make a daily structure where I know three days a week I'm writing a blog post or three days a week I'm doing a podcast or twice a week I'm doing a pair or whatever, again you don't have to do all of them, like I don't want you to overwhelm them, you gotta pick the ones that you like, the ones that are comfortable, the ones that you feel like you can thrive in and after you pick it you have to be consistent with it. Even though at first you're not gonna make any money along the way and you're like why am I doing this, nobody's listening to me, okay? Think about five years from now, think about 100,000 people in an auditorium or 100,000 or 100,000 people on a webinar, that's the key to this consistency, so I wanna kinda throw that out again. His question is, like, I've always thought about, like, I'm driving Facebook ad to Web and I registration page, right? And what you're probably doing right now is you're going after a warm audience and driving Facebook ad to Web and I registration page. That works, right? Because they're warm. Like, the bridge is very, very small. Until Facebook's, like, screw you, direct response marketer and kicks you out. But for right now, it works. There's not much more you need. But when you go outside of, like, your hot market, now we're stepping a step back and saying, hey, people that hate their job, now I'm taking to, like, let me tell you the number one reason why you probably hate your job, right? Now there's a quiz and it's like, did you know you could be certain, you know, and now you're taking through this thing where this cold person doesn't understand your market. That's what the pre-framed quiz is about. And then you dump them on Web and I registration page. Now, now you just open up this whole market to everyone who isn't already in your existing market. And now you don't just have to advertise on Tony Robbins' page. Now I can advertise on Geico Direct, right? Or whatever that is, because now it's like, it opens up this huge new world because now I know what their pain points are and I'm going to speak to them at that point. Does that kind of make sense?
